metric_name: Alpha XL 366 vs SGI Indigo 2 R440 — Pro/Engineer benchmark
metric_value: Alpha XL 366 MHz outperforms SGI Indigo 2 R440 (250 MHz) by 69% in Pro/Engineer benchmark
notes: SGI Indigo 2 priced at $33,000; Alpha XL at $17,413 — approximately half the cost

metric_name: Alpha XL 366 vs HP C110 — Pro/Engineer benchmark
metric_value: Alpha XL 366 MHz outperforms HP C110 Unix workstation by 81% in Pro/Engineer benchmark
notes: HP J210 priced at $34,980; Alpha XL at $17,413 — approximately half the cost with superior performance

metric_name: Digital Personal Workstation vs HP 712/100 — CADalyst benchmark
metric_value: Digital Personal Workstation outperforms HP 712/100 Unix workstation by 27% in CADalyst benchmark
notes: Windows NT Intel and Alpha CADalyst benchmarks both used

metric_name: Digital Personal Workstation vs Sun UltraSparc 1 — CADalyst benchmark
metric_value: Digital Personal Workstation outperforms Sun UltraSparc 1 by 60% in CADalyst benchmark
notes: CADalyst benchmark used for NT Intel and Alpha Personal Workstations

metric_name: Alpha XL vs SGI Indigo — Ansys benchmark
metric_value: Digital Alpha XL outperforms SGI Indigo by 42% in Ansys benchmark; HP J210 outperforms Alpha XL by 6%
notes: HP J210 at $34,980 edges Alpha XL at $17,413 by 6% — but at double the cost; price/performance strongly favors Digital

metric_name: Alpha XL vs Unix workstation price comparison
metric_value: Alpha XL listed at $17,413 vs SGI Indigo at $33,000 and HP J210 at $34,980 — approximately half the cost of comparable Unix workstations
notes: Price advantage was a core competitive differentiator; software costs also lower at PC price points

metric_name: NT applications available on Digital Personal Workstations
metric_value: 2,000+ NT native applications on Celebris XL Intel platform; 1,200+ on Alpha XL platform
notes: Intel applications ported first; Alpha had 1,200 due to Digital's aggressive ISV recruitment

metric_name: Personal Workstation fully configured cost vs Unix workstations
metric_value: Fully configured Personal Workstation with business/technical applications averages about half the cost of comparable Unix workstation products
notes: ISV software prices also dropped to PC price points for NT versions

metric_name: Personal Workstation target market 1: MDA
metric_value: Mechanical Design Automation (MDA): AutoDesk, Bentley, EDS, Parametric Technology, Matra-Datavision, SolidWorks
notes: Market 1 of 5 target markets; MCAD was largest opportunity vs low-end Unix workstations

metric_name: Personal Workstation target market 2: GIS
metric_value: Geographical Information Systems (GIS): 70+ software packages including ESRI, Bentley MicroStation Mapper, Cadcorp, ESC CEM, Small World GIS
notes: Market 2 of 5; 70+ GIS packages was strong application depth for this market

metric_name: Personal Workstation target market 3: Digital Content and Creation
metric_value: Digital Content and Creation (DCC): Adobe, Softimage, AutoDesk 3D Studio Max, Saros, Looking Glass — high-end publishing, web creation, animation
notes: Market 3 of 5; early evidence of NT penetrating media production market

metric_name: Personal Workstation target market 4: Financial
metric_value: Financial applications requiring powerful number-crunching or highly graphical display capabilities
notes: Market 4 of 5; financial workstation market was significant segment

metric_name: Personal Workstation target market 5: Software Development
metric_value: Software Development: compilers, GUI Builders, 4GL, CASE, Client/Server Application Development, OO tools — including ParcPlace Visual Works, Nutcracker, Informix SE
notes: Market 5 of 5; software developer workstations a key Unix-to-NT transition opportunity

metric_name: Personal Workstation indirect channel strategy
metric_value: Drive sales exclusively through VARs; internal sales force does NOT sell Personal Workstation but earns compensation for VAR referrals; no channel conflict
source_page: The New Digital — Distribution Through Indirect Channels
notes: Major strategic departure from DEC's traditional direct sales model

metric_name: Personal Workstation common platform strategy
metric_value: Single base chassis with three microprocessor families (Pentium, Pentium Pro, Alpha); economies of scale in manufacturing; field-upgradeable via daughter card
notes: Common platform reduced VAR inventory and training costs; key to VAR adoption

metric_name: Personal Workstation competitive market entry strategy
metric_value: Target low-end and mid-range of Unix workstation market with equivalent/superior performance at half the cost; use NT's growing ISV ecosystem as leverage
notes: Aberdeen described as Digital exploding into the low-end and mid-range Unix workstation marketplace

metric_name: Alpha XL dual personality positioning
metric_value: 1) Low-cost Unix workstation alternative; 2) Powerful NT-based commercial desktop; runs both PC apps and formerly Unix-only technical apps
notes: Dual positioning was strategic strength; addressed both cost-sensitive IT and technical workstation users

metric_name: 3Space Viewer bridging capability
metric_value: 3Space Viewer allows 3D technical designs to be imported into Microsoft Word/PowerPoint via OLE 2.0; images remain manipulable within Office documents
notes: Early example of technical-to-commercial workflow integration; included free with all Personal Workstations

metric_name: 64-bit application market exclusion
metric_value: Markets requiring 64-bit processing (GIS data warehousing, complex 3D EDA) will continue on Unix workstations; NT will not challenge 64-bit architectures for several years
notes: Accurate boundary condition; 64-bit Windows arrived 2005

metric_name: Alpha ISV porting status
metric_value: NT ISVs port to Intel first, then to platform with largest business opportunity; Digital secured second-key-platform porting status for Alpha ahead of PowerPC and MIPS
notes: NT ran on Intel, Alpha, PowerPC, MIPS; Alpha secured second place in ISV porting

metric_name: Personal Workstation Unix market disruption prediction
metric_value: Digital Personal Workstation is positioned to explode into the low-end and mid-range of the Unix workstation marketplace; HP and Sun face significant market share loss at low-end
notes: Aberdeen predicted fundamental Unix workstation market disruption from NT workstations

metric_name: Unix workstation market disruption — actual outcome
metric_value: Unix workstation market share collapsed through late 1990s-2000s as Windows NT workstations captured low-end and mid-range; SGI revenue declined from $2.5B in 1997 to bankruptcy filings in 2006 and 2009
notes: Aberdeen's Unix disruption prediction proved highly accurate; SGI filed for bankruptcy twice; HP and Sun workstation businesses severely contracted

metric_name: Personal Workstation VAR channel program viability
metric_value: Digital's VAR program will succeed as real differentiator: common base platform reduces VAR inventory/training costs; promotional margin programs incent VAR adoption
notes: Aberdeen gave strong marks to VAR program design; DEC's execution was cut short by 1998 acquisition

metric_name: Personal Workstation program outcome — actual outcome
metric_value: Digital acquired by Compaq January 1998; Personal Workstation product line absorbed; Alpha workstation line continued under Compaq/HP briefly; program did not achieve independent market leadership
notes: DEC acquisition by Compaq ended independent Personal Workstation program 18 months after publication

metric_name: Aberdeen assessment of Unix workstation competitive impact
metric_value: Personal Workstations should halve the cost of current low-end Unix workstations while providing superior price/performance; Unix workstation suppliers must cut costs or increase functionality
notes: Prediction of margin squeeze in Unix workstation market proved highly accurate

metric_name: Celebris XL product range
metric_value: Celebris XL Pentium: single/dual 100/133/150/166 MHz; Celebris XL Pentium Pro: single 150/180/200 MHz; shipped pre-configured with Windows NT
notes: Intel-based Personal Workstations targeted at volume market; Alpha for premium performance
